title: ED burn documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.
assessment.rationale: The exact DHA burn mechanism and classification section supports onset, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and injury mechanism only when discussed or assessed; it does not classify the burn automatically.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns
source.population: Adults and children with thermal, scald, chemical, electrical, friction, or inhalational burn presentations.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth burn documentation and escalation; definitive treatment remains clinician-led and serious burns require in-person or emergency assessment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports thermal, scald, electrical, chemical, friction, depth, blistering, redness, pain, site, and mechanism documentation.

assessment.text: Supports airway and inhalation assessment, neurological and neurovascular context, burn site, size or area, depth, blistering, circumferential features, and total-body-surface-area estimation.
assessment.rationale: The exact assessment section supports recording measured vital data, focused examination, injury severity and appearance only when clinician-assessed; no depth or area is inferred.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns
source.population: Adults and children with thermal, scald, chemical, electrical, friction, or inhalational burn presentations.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth burn documentation and escalation; definitive treatment remains clinician-led and serious burns require in-person or emergency assessment.

exact_location.heading: Section 7 — airway, vital signs, depth, site, size, and total body surface area

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports airway and inhalation assessment, neurological and neurovascular context, burn site, size or area, depth, blistering, circumferential features, and total-body-surface-area estimation.

assessment.text: Supports escalation for face or neck and airway involvement, inhalation, chemical or electrical burns, circumferential or deep burns, large surface area, special sites, comorbidity, infection, or associated trauma.
assessment.rationale: The exact red-flag and referral section supports documenting time-critical features and escalation only when assessed or decided.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Burns

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports escalation for face or neck and airway involvement, inhalation, chemical or electrical burns, circumferential or deep burns, large surface area, special sites, comorbidity, infection, or associated trauma.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-documented burn-care discussion, dressing, analgesia, tetanus, return precautions, infection monitoring, arranged follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment or dose selection.
assessment.rationale: The exact management and follow-up section supports recording only a clinician-entered plan and arranged handover or follow-up; treatment content is not generated.
